questions.prompt:
A researcher studies how evening light exposure affects melatonin, a hormone involved in sleep timing. Two groups of adults follow the same sleep schedule for one week. Group A uses dim room light for one hour before bed. Group B uses bright overhead light for one hour before bed. Melatonin level is measured at bedtime.
Group | Evening light condition | Average melatonin level (pg/mL)
A | Dim light | 48
B | Bright light | 26
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Bright evening light was associated with lower bedtime melatonin levels.
choices.B: Bright evening light increased bedtime melatonin levels.
choices.C: Light exposure had no relationship to melatonin level.
choices.D: Melatonin levels were identical in both groups.

questions.correctAnswerText: Bright evening light was associated with lower bedtime melatonin levels.
questions.explanation: The bright-light group had a lower average melatonin level than the dim-light group.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Impulse can be calculated as force × time.
Collision | Average force (N) | Contact time (s)
A | 80 | 0.5
B | 120 | 0.4
C | 60 | 1.0
D | 200 | 0.2
Which collision has the greatest impulse?

choices.A: Collision A
choices.B: Collision B
choices.C: Collision C
choices.D: Collision D

questions.correctAnswerText: Collision C
questions.explanation: Impulse = force × time. Collision C has 60 N × 1.0 s = 60 N·s, the greatest value.

questions.prompt:
A geologist studies pumice, a volcanic rock that contains many holes. These holes formed when gas bubbles were trapped in lava as it cooled quickly during an eruption. The rock is lightweight and has a glassy texture.
Which statement best describes how pumice forms?

choices.A: Lava cools quickly while gas bubbles are trapped inside.
choices.B: Sand grains are compacted at the bottom of a river.
choices.C: Existing rock is changed by heat and pressure without melting.
choices.D: Metal in Earth’s core solidifies into crystals.

questions.correctAnswerText: Lava cools quickly while gas bubbles are trapped inside.
questions.explanation: Pumice forms when gas bubbles are trapped in lava that cools quickly.

questions.prompt:
Ammonia is a toxic waste product formed when the body breaks down proteins. The liver converts ammonia into urea, a less toxic substance. Urea then travels in the blood to the kidneys, where it can be removed in urine.
Which statement best describes the role of the liver in this process?

choices.A: The liver changes a toxic waste into a less toxic substance.
choices.B: The liver pumps oxygen-rich blood to the lungs.
choices.C: The liver filters air before it enters the nose.
choices.D: The liver stores genetic information in chromosomes.

questions.correctAnswerText: The liver changes a toxic waste into a less toxic substance.
questions.explanation: The liver converts toxic ammonia into less toxic urea.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A person pushes a box with a force of 75 N for 4 meters.
Work = force × distance
How much work is done on the box in joules?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: Work = 75 N × 4 m = 300 J.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A student tests how the roughness of a surface affects the force needed to start moving a block. The same block is placed on four surfaces with different roughness levels. The student uses the same spring scale in every trial.
Which TWO factors should be kept constant?

choices.A: Mass of the block
choices.B: Spring scale used
choices.C: Roughness of the surface
choices.D: Force needed to start motion

questions.correctAnswerText: Mass of the block | Spring scale used
questions.explanation: These factors should stay constant so surface roughness is the main variable being tested.

questions.prompt: A trait is controlled by several genes, and environmental factors such as nutrition can also affect the trait. Human height is an example of [D1] inheritance because it is influenced by [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=polygenic; D2=multiple genes
questions.explanation: Polygenic traits are influenced by more than one gene.

questions.prompt:
A student uses a convex lens to focus sunlight onto a small spot on a card. The lens bends incoming light rays so they meet at a focal point.
Which property of light is mainly being used?

choices.A: Refraction
choices.B: Fermentation
choices.C: Blood clotting
choices.D: Rock compaction

questions.correctAnswerText: Refraction
questions.explanation: A convex lens focuses light by bending, or refracting, light rays.

questions.prompt:
A region depends heavily on groundwater for farming. Over several years, water is pumped from underground faster than it is naturally replaced. Wells must be drilled deeper, and some shallow wells stop producing water.
Which conclusion is best supported?

choices.A: Groundwater is being withdrawn faster than it is replenished.
choices.B: Groundwater cannot be affected by human use.
choices.C: Pumping water creates new aquifers instantly.
choices.D: Deeper wells prove that groundwater levels are rising.

questions.correctAnswerText: Groundwater is being withdrawn faster than it is replenished.
questions.explanation: Falling well levels and deeper drilling indicate groundwater is being used faster than it is replaced.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A clinic measures cortisol, a stress hormone, in volunteers before and after a relaxation program.
Group | Average cortisol before (µg/dL) | Average cortisol after (µg/dL)
Relaxation program | 18 | 12
No program | 17 | 16
Which claim is best supported by the data?

choices.A: The relaxation group showed a larger decrease in cortisol.
choices.B: The no-program group showed a larger decrease in cortisol.
choices.C: Cortisol increased in both groups.
choices.D: Cortisol stayed exactly the same in both groups.

questions.correctAnswerText: The relaxation group showed a larger decrease in cortisol.
questions.explanation: The relaxation group decreased by 6 µg/dL, compared with 1 µg/dL in the no-program group.

questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

tiles.B: Lift force measured by the sensor
tiles.C: Same fan speed used for every trial

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Shape of the wing model; T2=Lift force measured by the sensor; T3=Same fan speed used for every trial
questions.explanation: Wing shape is changed, lift force is measured, and fan speed is controlled.

questions.prompt:
Some neurons are covered by myelin, a fatty layer that helps electrical impulses travel faster along the axon. When myelin is damaged, nerve signals may slow down or fail to reach their target properly.
Which statement best describes the role of myelin?

choices.A: It helps nerve impulses travel efficiently along neurons.
choices.B: It digests food in the small intestine.
choices.C: It stores minerals in volcanic rock.
choices.D: It changes carbon dioxide into oxygen in the lungs.

questions.correctAnswerText: It helps nerve impulses travel efficiently along neurons.
questions.explanation: Myelin helps nerve impulses move quickly along axons.

questions.prompt:
A pendulum swings back and forth. At the highest point of the swing, the pendulum has more gravitational potential energy and less kinetic energy. At the lowest point, it moves fastest and has more kinetic energy.
Which statement best describes the energy change?

choices.A: Energy shifts between gravitational potential energy and kinetic energy.
choices.B: Energy is created only at the lowest point.
choices.C: Energy disappears at the highest point.
choices.D: Kinetic energy changes into DNA.

questions.correctAnswerText: Energy shifts between gravitational potential energy and kinetic energy.
questions.explanation: A pendulum changes energy form as height and speed change during the swing.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A solar farm produces 18,000 kilowatt-hours of energy in 6 hours.
What is the average energy production rate in kilowatt-hours per hour?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: 18,000 kilowatt-hours ÷ 6 hours = 3,000 kilowatt-hours per hour.

questions.prompt:
A researcher studies whether a high-fiber breakfast affects how full people feel before lunch. One group eats a high-fiber breakfast, and another group eats a low-fiber breakfast with the same number of calories. Both groups rate fullness after three hours.
Why should the breakfasts have the same number of calories?

choices.A: To help isolate fiber amount as the main variable being tested
choices.B: To make fiber and calories the same thing
choices.C: To prevent people from rating fullness
choices.D: To guarantee that both groups give identical answers

questions.correctAnswerText: To help isolate fiber amount as the main variable being tested
questions.explanation: Keeping calories the same helps test whether fiber amount affects fullness.

questions.prompt:
A student compares the strength of different bridge shapes made from the same number of craft sticks. Each bridge is placed between the same supports, and weights are added until the bridge bends 2 cm.
What is the dependent variable?

choices.A: Amount of weight needed to bend the bridge 2 cm
choices.B: Shape of the bridge
choices.C: Number of craft sticks used
choices.D: Distance between the supports

questions.correctAnswerText: Amount of weight needed to bend the bridge 2 cm
questions.explanation: The dependent variable is what is measured after changing bridge shape.

questions.prompt: A first-class lever has the fulcrum between the input force and the load. Moving the fulcrum closer to the load can [D1] the input force needed, but the input side must move through a [D2] distance.
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=decrease; D2=greater
questions.explanation: Moving the fulcrum closer to the load can reduce input force, but the input side must move farther.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A doctor measures urine concentration in a patient before and after the release of antidiuretic hormone (ADH). ADH causes the kidneys to reabsorb more water.
Condition | Urine volume in 4 hours (mL) | Urine concentration
Before ADH increase | 620 | Low
After ADH increase | 280 | High
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: After ADH increased, urine volume decreased and urine became more concentrated.
choices.B: After ADH increased, urine volume increased and urine became less concentrated.
choices.C: ADH caused the kidneys to stop interacting with water.
choices.D: Urine volume and concentration stayed the same.

questions.correctAnswerText: After ADH increased, urine volume decreased and urine became more concentrated.
questions.explanation: ADH causes more water reabsorption, resulting in lower urine volume and higher concentration.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student records the distance a braking cart travels after different brake-pad materials are used. The cart starts at the same speed in every trial.
Brake-pad material | Stopping distance (m)
Material A | 3.8
Material B | 2.9
Material C | 4.5
Material D | 2.1
Which material produced the shortest stopping distance?

choices.A: Material A
choices.B: Material B
choices.C: Material C
choices.D: Material D

questions.correctAnswerText: Material D
questions.explanation: Material D produced the shortest stopping distance, 2.1 m.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A state tracks electricity generated from two resources.
Year | Coal electricity (million MWh) | Wind electricity (million MWh)
1 | 92 | 18
2 | 81 | 27
3 | 70 | 39
4 | 61 | 52
Which TWO statements are best supported by the data?

choices.A: Coal electricity decreased from Year 1 to Year 4.
choices.B: Wind electricity increased from Year 1 to Year 4.
choices.C: Coal electricity increased every year.
choices.D: Wind electricity was highest in Year 1.
choices.E: Both resources produced exactly the same amount every year.

questions.correctAnswerText: Coal electricity decreased from Year 1 to Year 4. | Wind electricity increased from Year 1 to Year 4.
questions.explanation: Coal decreased from 92 to 61 million MWh, while wind increased from 18 to 52 million MWh.

questions.prompt:
A vaccine causes the immune system to produce memory cells. Years later, if the same pathogen enters the body, memory cells help white blood cells respond more quickly and produce antibodies faster.
Which statement best describes the role of memory cells?

choices.A: They help the immune system respond faster to a later exposure.
choices.B: They pump blood through the heart.
choices.C: They form lava during volcanic eruptions.
choices.D: They prevent cells from making proteins.

questions.correctAnswerText: They help the immune system respond faster to a later exposure.
questions.explanation: Memory cells help the immune system respond more quickly if the same pathogen appears again.

questions.prompt:
A student places a book on a desk. Gravity pulls the book downward, and the desk pushes upward with an equal force. The book remains still because the forces cancel.
Which statement best describes the forces?

choices.A: The forces are balanced.
choices.B: The downward force is the only force.
choices.C: The book must accelerate upward.
choices.D: The desk force removes the book’s mass.

questions.correctAnswerText: The forces are balanced.
questions.explanation: Equal upward and downward forces cancel, so the book remains still.

questions.prompt:
A text model describes how large mountain ranges can form. Two continental plates slowly move toward each other. Because both plates are relatively thick and buoyant, neither plate easily sinks. The crust folds, thickens, and rises over millions of years.
Which landform is most likely produced by this process?

choices.A: Folded mountain range
choices.B: Ocean trench made by oceanic subduction
choices.C: River delta formed from deposited sediment
choices.D: Crater formed by a meteor impact

questions.correctAnswerText: Folded mountain range
questions.explanation: Continental collision can fold and thicken crust, forming mountain ranges.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A study follows 360 adults. Of these adults, 54 have a family history of a certain inherited disorder.
What percent of the adults have a family history of the disorder?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.

A student tests how the angle of a solar panel affects energy output. The panel is placed at four different angles on the same sunny day. The same panel and same measuring device are used each time.
Which factor is the independent variable?

choices.A: Angle of the solar panel
choices.B: Energy output
choices.C: Same solar panel
choices.D: Same measuring device

questions.correctAnswerText: Angle of the solar panel
questions.explanation: The independent variable is the factor intentionally changed by the student.

questions.prompt:
The rough endoplasmic reticulum has ribosomes attached to its surface. Proteins made by these ribosomes may enter the rough endoplasmic reticulum, where they begin to fold and be prepared for transport.
Which statement best describes the rough endoplasmic reticulum?

choices.A: It helps process proteins made by attached ribosomes.
choices.B: It stores most groundwater in aquifers.
choices.C: It changes oxygen into rock crystals.
choices.D: It produces earthquakes at plate boundaries.

questions.correctAnswerText: It helps process proteins made by attached ribosomes.
questions.explanation: Rough endoplasmic reticulum helps fold and process proteins made by ribosomes.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student records the output of a hand-crank generator.
Crank turns per minute: 20, 40, 60, 80
Energy produced in 1 min (J): 15, 31, 45, 62
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: More crank turns per minute were associated with more energy produced.
choices.B: More crank turns per minute were associated with less energy produced.
choices.C: Energy produced stayed exactly the same at all crank speeds.
choices.D: Cranking had no relationship to energy output.

questions.correctAnswerText: More crank turns per minute were associated with more energy produced.
questions.explanation: Energy output increased as crank turns per minute increased.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student compares four rocky objects in space.
Object | Diameter (km)
A | 12
B | 48
C | 520
D | 1,200
Which object has the largest diameter?

choices.A: Object A
choices.B: Object B
choices.C: Object C
choices.D: Object D

questions.correctAnswerText: Object D
questions.explanation: Object D has the largest listed diameter, 1,200 km.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A neurologist compares average nerve signal speed in two groups.
Group | Average nerve signal speed (m/s)
Healthy myelin | 85
Damaged myelin | 34
Which claim is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Damaged myelin was associated with slower nerve signal speed.
choices.B: Damaged myelin was associated with faster nerve signal speed.
choices.C: Myelin condition had no relationship to nerve signal speed.
choices.D: Both groups had exactly the same nerve signal speed.

questions.correctAnswerText: Damaged myelin was associated with slower nerve signal speed.
questions.explanation: The damaged-myelin group had a lower average signal speed than the healthy-myelin group.

questions.prompt:
A student mixes an acidic cleaner with a basic cleaner and notices that the mixture becomes warmer. The labels warn not to mix the cleaners because the reaction can release heat and produce harmful fumes.
Which statement best describes the heat change?

choices.A: The reaction is exothermic because thermal energy is released.
choices.B: The reaction is endothermic because thermal energy is released.
choices.C: The reaction is not chemical because the mixture becomes warm.
choices.D: The reaction stops all energy transfer.

questions.correctAnswerText: The reaction is exothermic because thermal energy is released.
questions.explanation: A reaction that releases thermal energy is exothermic.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A researcher studies whether a new balance board improves ankle stability. Participants use the board for different practice times each day. Ankle stability is measured with the same balance test after four weeks.
Which TWO steps would improve the investigation?

choices.A: Use the same balance test for all participants.
choices.B: Include multiple participants at each practice-time level.
choices.C: Change the balance test for each participant.
choices.D: Record only participants with the best scores.
choices.E: Use different study lengths for different participants.

questions.correctAnswerText: Use the same balance test for all participants. | Include multiple participants at each practice-time level.
questions.explanation: A consistent test and repeated participants at each level improve reliability.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A machine does 360 J of work in 12 seconds.
Power = work ÷ time
What is the machine’s power?
questions.explanation: Power = 360 J ÷ 12 s = 30 W.

questions.prompt:
A forest is damaged by a windstorm that knocks down many trees but leaves soil and some roots in place. In the following years, grasses and shrubs grow quickly, and tree seedlings begin to appear.
Which ecological process is most directly described?

choices.A: Secondary succession
choices.B: Lunar eclipse
choices.C: Chemical neutralization
choices.D: Mechanical advantage

questions.correctAnswerText: Secondary succession
questions.explanation: Secondary succession occurs after a disturbance when soil remains.

questions.prompt:
A person inherits one changed copy of a gene involved in DNA repair. The changed copy does not guarantee disease, but it can increase the person’s risk for certain cancers because damaged DNA may be repaired less effectively.
Which statement is best supported?

choices.A: Some inherited gene changes can increase disease risk without guaranteeing disease.
choices.B: Inherited gene changes never affect health.
choices.C: DNA repair genes are found only in bacteria.
choices.D: A changed gene always prevents all cell division.

questions.correctAnswerText: Some inherited gene changes can increase disease risk without guaranteeing disease.
questions.explanation: Some mutations increase risk by affecting repair processes, but they do not guarantee a disease will occur.
